events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.92,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Flashing installed without required laps and seals at joints",
    "Unlapped/unsealed flashing creates a direct water-intrusion path behind the masonry veneer",
    "Missing end laps and sealant violate TMS 402 flashing continuity requirements",
    "BIA tech notes require flashing to be lapped a minimum of 4 inches and sealed at joints to maintain a continuous moisture barrier"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop further masonry closure above affected flashing until laps and seals are corrected",
    "Rework all flashing joints to provide required minimum laps and continuous sealant per TMS 402 and BIA tech notes",
    "Inspect and document all flashing runs for lap continuity and seal before proceeding",
    "Hold a pre-closeout verification to confirm flashing system is watertight"
  ],
  "rationale": "Flashing that is not lapped and sealed fails to provide the continuous moisture barrier required by TMS 402 and BIA technical notes. At rough-complete stage this is a correctable but serious defect — if closed in without remediation, water will bypass the flashing and enter the wall assembly, causing concealed damage. The work is non-compliant and must be reworked before closure.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402 — Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures (flashing continuity and lap requirements)",
    "BIA Technical Notes 7A — Water Resistance of Brick Masonry (flashing installation, laps, and seals)"
  ]
}
